title: bursera bonetii
intro: Bursera bonetii, also known as the Pino or Palo Fierro tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Burseraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its resin and leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Bursera bonetii include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and infections. Herbal preparations of Bursera bonetii are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
